meta.source_text: Pearl of Great Price, The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints

preamble.summary: Moses 6 covers the genealogy from Adam to Enoch, the institution of a book of remembrance and a written language, the doctrine of the Fall and redemption taught to Adam, Adam's baptism, and the calling and early ministry of Enoch. It parallels portions of Genesis 5 but massively expands the theological content.
preamble.remarkable: Smith introduces the first written language as divinely given (6:5-6), a 'book of remembrance' kept from Adam's time, and the doctrine of original guilt explicitly rejected: 'the Son of God hath atoned for original guilt' so that 'the sins of the parents cannot be answered upon the heads of the children' (6:54). Adam is baptized (6:64-66) and receives the priesthood. Enoch's call (6:26-36) includes the famous exchange: God weeps, and Enoch asks 'How is it that thou canst weep?' (later in ch. 7).
preamble.friction: Genesis 5 is a bare genealogy with no theology. Smith transforms it into a full doctrinal treatise. The rejection of original guilt contradicts traditional Augustinian doctrine. Baptism in Adam's era is unknown to any biblical tradition. The book of remembrance and the concept of a divinely given written language have no Genesis parallel.
preamble.connections: The genealogy parallels Genesis 5:1-32. The rejection of inherited guilt connects to Article of Faith 2 and Moroni 8:8. Adam's baptism connects to D&C 20:25-26. The book of remembrance connects to Malachi 3:16. Enoch's call anticipates Moses 7.

verses.text_source: And Adam hearkened unto the voice of God, and called upon his sons to repent.
verses.rendering: And Adam hearkened unto the voice of God, and called upon his sons to repent.
translator_notes: Transitional verse from Moses 5. Adam continues his prophetic ministry.

verses.text_source: And Adam knew his wife again, and she bare a son, and he called his name Seth. And Adam glorified the name of God; for he said: God hath appointed me another seed, instead of Abel, whom Cain slew.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 4:25 — And Adam knew his wife again; and she bare a son, and called his name Seth: For God, said she, hath appointed me another seed instead of Abel, whom Cain slew.
verses.rendering: And Adam knew his wife again, and she bare a son, and he called his name Seth. And Adam glorified the name of God; for he said: God hath appointed me another seed, instead of Abel, whom Cain slew.
translator_notes: Follows Gen 4:25. Adam (not Eve as in KJV) names Seth and praises God. The replacement-seed theology is retained.

verses.text_source: And God revealed himself unto Seth, and he rebelled not, but offered an acceptable sacrifice, like unto his brother Abel. And to him also was born a son, and he called his name Enos.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 4:26a — And to Seth, to him also there was born a son; and he called his name Enos.
verses.rendering: And God revealed himself unto Seth, and he rebelled not, but offered an acceptable sacrifice, like unto his brother Abel. And to him also was born a son, and he called his name Enos.
translator_notes: Adds Seth's personal revelation from God and his acceptable sacrifice. Seth is positioned as Abel's successor in righteousness.

verses.text_source: And then began these men to call upon the name of the Lord, and the Lord blessed them;
verses.text_reference: Genesis 4:26b — then began men to call upon the name of the LORD.
verses.rendering: And then began these men to call upon the name of the Lord, and the Lord blessed them;
translator_notes: Follows Gen 4:26b. 'These men' specifies Seth's lineage, not humanity generally. God's blessing is added.

verses.text_source: And a book of remembrance was kept, in the which was recorded, in the language of Adam, for it was given unto as many as called upon God to write by the spirit of inspiration;
verses.rendering: And a book of remembrance was kept, in the which was recorded, in the language of Adam, for it was given unto as many as called upon God to write by the spirit of inspiration;
translator_notes: No Genesis parallel. A 'book of remembrance' is kept from the beginning. Writing is divinely given — the 'language of Adam' is a pure, original language. Cf. Malachi 3:16.

verses.text_source: And by them their children were taught to read and write, having a language which was pure and undefiled.
verses.rendering: And by them their children were taught to read and write, having a language which was pure and undefiled.
translator_notes: Literacy is ancient and divinely originated. The Adamic language is 'pure and undefiled' — a concept connecting to Zephaniah 3:9.

verses.text_source: Now this same Priesthood, which was in the beginning, shall be in the end of the world also.
verses.rendering: Now this same Priesthood, which was in the beginning, shall be in the end of the world also.
translator_notes: No Genesis parallel. The priesthood is eternal — present from Adam and continuing to the last days. This is a foundational LDS priesthood doctrine.

verses.text_source: Now this prophecy Adam spake, as he was moved upon by the Holy Ghost, and a genealogy was kept of the children of God. And this was the book of the generations of Adam, saying: In the day that God created man, in the likeness of God made he him;
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:1 — This is the book of the generations of Adam. In the day that God created man, in the likeness of God made he him;
verses.rendering: Now this prophecy Adam spake, as he was moved upon by the Holy Ghost, and a genealogy was kept of the children of God. And this was the book of the generations of Adam, saying: In the day that God created man, in the likeness of God made he him;
translator_notes: Expands Gen 5:1 with the note that Adam prophesied by the Holy Ghost and that a genealogy was maintained of 'the children of God.'

verses.text_source: In the image of his own body, male and female, created he them, and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created and became living souls in the land upon the footstool of God.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:2 — Male and female created he them; and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created.
verses.rendering: In the image of his own body, male and female, created he them, and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created and became living souls in the land upon the footstool of God.
translator_notes: 'In the image of his own body' — Smith makes the imago Dei corporeal. 'Became living souls in the land upon the footstool of God' — the earth is God's footstool (cf. Isaiah 66:1).

verses.text_source: And Adam lived one hundred and thirty years, and begat a son in his own likeness, after his own image, and called his name Seth.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:3 — And Adam lived an hundred and thirty years, and begat a son in his own likeness, after his image; and called his name Seth:
verses.rendering: And Adam lived one hundred and thirty years, and begat a son in his own likeness, after his own image, and called his name Seth.
translator_notes: Follows Gen 5:3.

verses.text_source: And the days of Adam, after he had begotten Seth, were eight hundred years, and he begat many sons and daughters;
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:4 — And the days of Adam after he had begotten Seth were eight hundred years: and he begat sons and daughters:
verses.rendering: And the days of Adam, after he had begotten Seth, were eight hundred years, and he begat many sons and daughters;
translator_notes: Follows Gen 5:4.

verses.text_source: And all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years, and he died.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:5 — And all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years: and he died.
verses.rendering: And all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years, and he died.
translator_notes: Identical to Gen 5:5.

verses.text_source: Seth lived one hundred and five years, and begat Enos, and prophesied in all his days, and taught his son Enos in the ways of God; wherefore Enos prophesied also.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:6 — And Seth lived an hundred and five years, and begat Enos:
verses.rendering: Seth lived one hundred and five years, and begat Enos, and prophesied in all his days, and taught his son Enos in the ways of God; wherefore Enos prophesied also.
translator_notes: Expands Gen 5:6 — Seth prophesied and taught Enos, who also prophesied. The genealogy becomes a chain of prophets.

verses.text_source: And Seth lived, after he begat Enos, eight hundred and seven years, and begat many sons and daughters.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:7 — And Seth lived after he begat Enos eight hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters:
verses.rendering: And Seth lived, after he begat Enos, eight hundred and seven years, and begat many sons and daughters.
translator_notes: Follows Gen 5:7.

verses.text_source: And the children of men were numerous upon all the face of the land. And in those days Satan had great dominion among men, and raged in their hearts; and from thenceforth came wars and bloodshed; and a man's hand was against his own brother, in administering death, because of secret works, seeking for power.
verses.rendering: And the children of men were numerous upon all the face of the land. And in those days Satan had great dominion among men, and raged in their hearts; and from thenceforth came wars and bloodshed; and a man's hand was against his own brother, in administering death, because of secret works, seeking for power.
translator_notes: No Genesis parallel. A summary of antediluvian violence: Satan's dominion, wars, bloodshed, and murder for power — all flowing from the secret combinations of Moses 5.

verses.text_source: And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years, and he died.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:8 — And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years: and he died.
verses.rendering: And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years, and he died.
translator_notes: Identical to Gen 5:8.

verses.text_source: And Enos lived ninety years, and begat Cainan. And Enos and the residue of the people of God came out from the land, which was called Shulon, and dwelt in a land of promise, which he called after his own son, whom he had named Cainan.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:9 — And Enos lived ninety years, and begat Cainan:
verses.rendering: And Enos lived ninety years, and begat Cainan. And Enos and the residue of the people of God came out from the land, which was called Shulon, and dwelt in a land of promise, which he called after his own son, whom he had named Cainan.
translator_notes: Expands Gen 5:9 with a migration narrative. 'The people of God' separate from the wicked and settle in a 'land of promise' named Cainan.

verses.text_source: And Enos lived, after he begat Cainan, eight hundred and fifteen years, and begat many sons and daughters. And all the days of Enos were nine hundred and five years, and he died.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:10-11 — And Enos lived after he begat Cainan eight hundred and fifteen years, and begat sons and daughters: And all the days of Enos were nine hundred and five years: and he died.
verses.rendering: And Enos lived, after he begat Cainan, eight hundred and fifteen years, and begat many sons and daughters. And all the days of Enos were nine hundred and five years, and he died.
translator_notes: Combines Gen 5:10-11.

verses.text_source: And Cainan lived seventy years, and begat Mahalaleel; and Cainan lived after he begat Mahalaleel eight hundred and forty years, and begat sons and daughters. And all the days of Cainan were nine hundred and ten years, and he died.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:12-14
verses.rendering: And Cainan lived seventy years, and begat Mahalaleel; and Cainan lived after he begat Mahalaleel eight hundred and forty years, and begat sons and daughters. And all the days of Cainan were nine hundred and ten years, and he died.
translator_notes: Combines Gen 5:12-14. Standard genealogical formula.

verses.text_source: And Mahalaleel lived sixty-five years, and begat Jared; and Mahalaleel lived, after he begat Jared, eight hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters. And all the days of Mahalaleel were eight hundred and ninety-five years, and he died.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:15-17
verses.rendering: And Mahalaleel lived sixty-five years, and begat Jared; and Mahalaleel lived, after he begat Jared, eight hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters. And all the days of Mahalaleel were eight hundred and ninety-five years, and he died.
translator_notes: Combines Gen 5:15-17.

verses.text_source: And Jared lived one hundred and sixty-two years, and begat Enoch; and Jared lived, after he begat Enoch, eight hundred years, and begat sons and daughters. And Jared taught Enoch in all the ways of God.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:18-19
verses.rendering: And Jared lived one hundred and sixty-two years, and begat Enoch; and Jared lived, after he begat Enoch, eight hundred years, and begat sons and daughters. And Jared taught Enoch in all the ways of God.
translator_notes: Expands Gen 5:18-19 with the note that Jared taught Enoch 'in all the ways of God' — establishing Enoch's training before his prophetic call.

verses.text_source: And this is the genealogy of the sons of Adam, who was the son of God, with whom God, himself, conversed.
verses.rendering: And this is the genealogy of the sons of Adam, who was the son of God, with whom God, himself, conversed.
translator_notes: Adam is called 'the son of God' — a title connecting to Luke 3:38. God conversed with Adam personally.

verses.text_source: And they were preachers of righteousness, and spake and prophesied, and called upon all men, everywhere, to repent; and faith was taught unto the children of men.
verses.rendering: And they were preachers of righteousness, and spake and prophesied, and called upon all men, everywhere, to repent; and faith was taught unto the children of men.
translator_notes: The entire Sethite line are 'preachers of righteousness' — a title applied to Noah in 2 Peter 2:5. Universal call to repentance.

verses.text_source: And it came to pass that all the days of Jared were nine hundred and sixty-two years, and he died.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:20 — And all the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty and two years: and he died.
verses.rendering: And it came to pass that all the days of Jared were nine hundred and sixty-two years, and he died.
translator_notes: Follows Gen 5:20.

verses.text_source: And Enoch lived sixty-five years, and begat Methuselah.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:21 — And Enoch lived sixty and five years, and begat Methuselah:
verses.rendering: And Enoch lived sixty-five years, and begat Methuselah.
translator_notes: Follows Gen 5:21.

verses.text_source: And it came to pass that Enoch journeyed in the land, among the people, standing upon the hills and the high places, and cried with a loud voice unto the people, saying: Repent, for thus saith the Lord: I am angry with this people, and my fierce anger is kindled against them; for their hearts have waxed hard, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes cannot see afar off;
verses.rendering: And it came to pass that Enoch journeyed in the land, among the people, standing upon the hills and the high places, and cried with a loud voice unto the people, saying: Repent, for thus saith the Lord: I am angry with this people, and my fierce anger is kindled against them; for their hearts have waxed hard, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes cannot see afar off;
translator_notes: No Genesis parallel. Enoch's prophetic ministry begins. He preaches from hilltops. The people's spiritual blindness echoes Isaiah 6:10 and Matthew 13:15.

verses.text_source: And for these many generations, ever since the day that I created them, have they gone astray, and have denied me, and have sought their own counsels in the dark; and in their own abominations have they devised murder, and have not kept the commandments, which I gave unto their father, Adam.
verses.rendering: And for these many generations, ever since the day that I created them, have they gone astray, and have denied me, and have sought their own counsels in the dark; and in their own abominations have they devised murder, and have not kept the commandments, which I gave unto their father, Adam.
translator_notes: God's indictment: generations of apostasy, denial, dark counsels, murder, and disobedience — all traceable to the secret combinations of Moses 5.

verses.text_source: Wherefore, they have foresworn themselves, and, by their oaths, they have brought upon themselves death; and a hell I have prepared for them, if they repent not;
verses.rendering: Wherefore, they have foresworn themselves, and, by their oaths, they have brought upon themselves death; and a hell I have prepared for them, if they repent not;
translator_notes: The satanic oaths bring death. Hell is prepared conditionally — 'if they repent not.'

verses.text_source: And this is a decree, which I have sent forth in the beginning of the world, from my own mouth, from the foundation thereof, and by the mouths of my servants, thy fathers, have I decreed it, even as it shall be sent forth in the world, unto the ends thereof.
verses.rendering: And this is a decree, which I have sent forth in the beginning of the world, from my own mouth, from the foundation thereof, and by the mouths of my servants, thy fathers, have I decreed it, even as it shall be sent forth in the world, unto the ends thereof.
translator_notes: The decree of judgment and repentance has been proclaimed since the world's foundation through the patriarchal prophets.

verses.text_source: And when Enoch had heard these words, he bowed himself to the earth, before the Lord, and spake before the Lord, saying: Why is it that I have found favor in thy sight, and am but a lad, and all the people hate me; for I am slow of speech; wherefore am I thy servant?
verses.rendering: And when Enoch had heard these words, he bowed himself to the earth, before the Lord, and spake before the Lord, saying: Why is it that I have found favor in thy sight, and am but a lad, and all the people hate me; for I am slow of speech; wherefore am I thy servant?
translator_notes: Enoch's reluctance parallels Moses (Exodus 4:10) and Jeremiah (Jer 1:6). He is young ('a lad'), unpopular, and 'slow of speech.'

verses.text_source: And the Lord said unto Enoch: Go forth and do as I have commanded thee, and no man shall pierce thee. Open thy mouth, and it shall be filled, and I will give thee utterance, for all flesh is in my hands, and I will do as seemeth me good.
verses.rendering: And the Lord said unto Enoch: Go forth and do as I have commanded thee, and no man shall pierce thee. Open thy mouth, and it shall be filled, and I will give thee utterance, for all flesh is in my hands, and I will do as seemeth me good.
translator_notes: God's response to Enoch's reluctance: protection ('no man shall pierce thee'), inspired speech ('I will give thee utterance'), and sovereignty ('all flesh is in my hands'). Echoes Jeremiah 1:7-8.

verses.text_source: Say unto this people: Choose ye this day, to serve the Lord God who made you.
verses.rendering: Say unto this people: Choose ye this day, to serve the Lord God who made you.
translator_notes: Echoes Joshua 24:15 ('choose you this day whom ye will serve'). Agency remains central.

verses.text_source: Behold my Spirit is upon you, wherefore all thy words will I justify; and the mountains shall flee before you, and the rivers shall turn from their course; and thou shalt abide in me, and I in you; therefore walk with me.
verses.rendering: Behold my Spirit is upon you, wherefore all thy words will I justify; and the mountains shall flee before you, and the rivers shall turn from their course; and thou shalt abide in me, and I in you; therefore walk with me.
translator_notes: Extraordinary promises: nature obeys Enoch, and the mutual indwelling language ('thou shalt abide in me, and I in you') echoes John 15:4. 'Walk with me' connects to Gen 5:22 ('Enoch walked with God').

verses.text_source: And the Lord spake unto Enoch, and said unto him: Anoint thine eyes with clay, and wash them, and thou shalt see. And he did so.
verses.rendering: And the Lord spake unto Enoch, and said unto him: Anoint thine eyes with clay, and wash them, and thou shalt see. And he did so.
translator_notes: A vision-enabling ritual. Echoes Jesus healing the blind man with clay (John 9:6-7). Enoch's spiritual sight is physically enacted.

verses.text_source: And he beheld the spirits that God had created; and he beheld also things which were not visible to the natural eye; and from thenceforth came the saying abroad in the land: A seer hath the Lord raised up unto his people.
verses.rendering: And he beheld the spirits that God had created; and he beheld also things which were not visible to the natural eye; and from thenceforth came the saying abroad in the land: A seer hath the Lord raised up unto his people.
translator_notes: Enoch sees the spirit world. He is designated a 'seer' — a term defined in Mosiah 8:13-17 as greater than a prophet.

verses.text_source: And it came to pass that Enoch went forth in the land, among the people, standing upon the hills and the high places, and cried with a loud voice, testifying against their works; and all men were offended because of him.
verses.rendering: And it came to pass that Enoch went forth in the land, among the people, standing upon the hills and the high places, and cried with a loud voice, testifying against their works; and all men were offended because of him.
translator_notes: Enoch's public ministry provokes universal offense. The prophet as social irritant.

verses.text_source: And they came forth to hear him, upon the high places, saying unto the tent-keepers: Tarry ye here and keep the tents, while we go yonder to behold the seer, for he prophesieth, and there is a strange thing in the land; a wild man hath come among us.
verses.rendering: And they came forth to hear him, upon the high places, saying unto the tent-keepers: Tarry ye here and keep the tents, while we go yonder to behold the seer, for he prophesieth, and there is a strange thing in the land; a wild man hath come among us.
translator_notes: The people are curious but dismissive — they call Enoch 'a wild man.' The detail of tent-keepers indicates a semi-nomadic society.

verses.text_source: And all men were offended because of him; and they came unto him and said: Tell us plainly who thou art, and from whence thou comest?
verses.rendering: And all men were offended because of him; and they came unto him and said: Tell us plainly who thou art, and from whence thou comest?
translator_notes: The people demand Enoch identify himself — a challenge to his authority.

verses.text_source: And he said unto them: I came out from the land of Cainan, the land of my fathers, a land of righteousness unto this day. And my father taught me in all the ways of God.
verses.rendering: And he said unto them: I came out from the land of Cainan, the land of my fathers, a land of righteousness unto this day. And my father taught me in all the ways of God.
translator_notes: Enoch identifies his origin: the righteous Sethite settlement of Cainan (Moses 6:17). His father Jared trained him.

verses.text_source: And it came to pass, as I journeyed from the land of Cainan, by the sea east, I beheld a vision; and lo, the heavens I saw, and the Lord spake with me, and gave me commandment; wherefore, for this cause, to keep the commandment, I speak forth these words.
verses.rendering: And it came to pass, as I journeyed from the land of Cainan, by the sea east, I beheld a vision; and lo, the heavens I saw, and the Lord spake with me, and gave me commandment; wherefore, for this cause, to keep the commandment, I speak forth these words.
translator_notes: Enoch describes his call vision — it occurred during a journey. The authority for his preaching is divine commandment.

verses.text_source: And Enoch continued his speech, saying: The Lord which spake with me, the same is the God of heaven, and he is my God, and your God, and ye are my brethren, and why counsel ye yourselves, and deny the God of heaven?
verses.rendering: And Enoch continued his speech, saying: The Lord which spake with me, the same is the God of heaven, and he is my God, and your God, and ye are my brethren, and why counsel ye yourselves, and deny the God of heaven?
translator_notes: Enoch claims common brotherhood and a common God. 'Why counsel ye yourselves' — they follow their own counsel rather than God's.

verses.text_source: The heavens he made; the earth is his footstool; and the foundation thereof is his. Behold, he laid it, an host of men hath he brought in upon the face thereof.
verses.rendering: The heavens he made; the earth is his footstool; and the foundation thereof is his. Behold, he laid it, an host of men hath he brought in upon the face thereof.
translator_notes: Echoes Isaiah 66:1 ('the earth is my footstool'). God is creator and populator of the earth.

verses.text_source: And death hath come upon our fathers; nevertheless we know them, and cannot deny, and even the first of all we know, even Adam.
verses.rendering: And death hath come upon our fathers; nevertheless we know them, and cannot deny, and even the first of all we know, even Adam.
translator_notes: Enoch appeals to the chain of testimony from Adam down. Adam is personally known to this generation (given the long lifespans of the genealogy).

verses.text_source: For a book of remembrance we have written among us, according to the pattern given by the finger of God; and it is given in our own language.
verses.rendering: For a book of remembrance we have written among us, according to the pattern given by the finger of God; and it is given in our own language.
translator_notes: The book of remembrance is cited as evidence. 'The finger of God' gave the pattern — an allusion to the tablets of Moses (Exodus 31:18).

verses.text_source: And as Enoch spake forth the words of God, the people trembled, and could not stand in his presence.
verses.rendering: And as Enoch spake forth the words of God, the people trembled, and could not stand in his presence.
translator_notes: Enoch's words carry divine power — the people physically tremble. The 'slow of speech' lad now speaks with overwhelming authority.

verses.text_source: And he said unto them: Because that Adam fell, we are; and by his fall came death; and we are made partakers of misery and woe.
verses.rendering: And he said unto them: Because that Adam fell, we are; and by his fall came death; and we are made partakers of misery and woe.
translator_notes: The Fall doctrine in brief: existence ('we are') depends on the Fall, but death, misery, and woe are its consequences.

verses.text_source: Behold Satan hath come among the children of men, and tempteth them to worship him; and men have become carnal, sensual, and devilish, and are shut out from the presence of God.
verses.rendering: Behold Satan hath come among the children of men, and tempteth them to worship him; and men have become carnal, sensual, and devilish, and are shut out from the presence of God.
translator_notes: Echoes Moses 5:13. 'Carnal, sensual, and devilish' is the recurring description of fallen human nature.

verses.text_source: But God hath made known unto our fathers that all men must repent.
verses.rendering: But God hath made known unto our fathers that all men must repent.
translator_notes: Repentance is universal — no one is exempt.

verses.text_source: And he called upon our father Adam by his own voice, saying: I am God; I made the world, and men before they were in the flesh.
verses.rendering: And he called upon our father Adam by his own voice, saying: I am God; I made the world, and men before they were in the flesh.
translator_notes: God's self-identification to Adam. 'Men before they were in the flesh' — the pre-mortal existence of spirits, a core LDS doctrine.

verses.text_source: And he also said unto him: If thou wilt turn unto me, and hearken unto my voice, and believe, and repent of all thy transgressions, and be baptized, even in water, in the name of mine Only Begotten Son, who is full of grace and truth, which is Jesus Christ, the only name which shall be given under heaven, whereby salvation shall come unto the children of men, ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost, asking all things in his name, and whatsoever ye shall ask, it shall be given you.
verses.rendering: And he also said unto him: If thou wilt turn unto me, and hearken unto my voice, and believe, and repent of all thy transgressions, and be baptized, even in water, in the name of mine Only Begotten Son, who is full of grace and truth, which is Jesus Christ, the only name which shall be given under heaven, whereby salvation shall come unto the children of men, ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost, asking all things in his name, and whatsoever ye shall ask, it shall be given you.
translator_notes: The full gospel plan given to Adam: faith, repentance, baptism in water, in the name of Jesus Christ, and the gift of the Holy Ghost. Christ is named explicitly. Echoes Acts 4:12 and 2 Nephi 31:13-17.

verses.text_source: And our father Adam spake unto the Lord, and said: Why is it that men must repent and be baptized in water? And the Lord said unto Adam: Behold I have forgiven thee thy transgression in the Garden of Eden.
verses.rendering: And our father Adam spake unto the Lord, and said: Why is it that men must repent and be baptized in water? And the Lord said unto Adam: Behold I have forgiven thee thy transgression in the Garden of Eden.
translator_notes: Adam asks the purpose of baptism. God's answer begins with forgiveness — Adam's original transgression is already forgiven.

verses.text_source: Hence came the saying abroad among the people, that the Son of God hath atoned for original guilt, wherein the sins of the parents cannot be answered upon the heads of the children, for they are whole from the foundation of the world.
verses.rendering: Hence came the saying abroad among the people, that the Son of God hath atoned for original guilt, wherein the sins of the parents cannot be answered upon the heads of the children, for they are whole from the foundation of the world.
translator_notes: A formal rejection of inherited guilt / original sin. Children are 'whole from the foundation of the world.' The Atonement covers Adamic transgression universally. Cf. Article of Faith 2, Moroni 8:8.

verses.text_source: And the Lord spake unto Adam, saying: Inasmuch as thy children are conceived in sin, even so when they begin to grow up, sin conceiveth in their hearts, and they taste the bitter, that they may know to prize the good.
verses.rendering: And the Lord spake unto Adam, saying: Inasmuch as thy children are conceived in sin, even so when they begin to grow up, sin conceiveth in their hearts, and they taste the bitter, that they may know to prize the good.
translator_notes: 'Conceived in sin' refers to being born into a sinful world, not to inherited guilt (which was just rejected). Children learn to sin as they grow. Tasting bitter is necessary to prize the good — the opposition principle of 2 Nephi 2:11.

verses.text_source: And it is given unto them to know good from evil; wherefore they are agents unto themselves, and I have given unto you another law and commandment.
verses.rendering: And it is given unto them to know good from evil; wherefore they are agents unto themselves, and I have given unto you another law and commandment.
translator_notes: Moral knowledge produces agency. 'Agents unto themselves' — a key LDS phrase for moral autonomy. A new commandment follows.

verses.text_source: Wherefore teach it unto your children, that all men, everywhere, must repent, or they can in nowise inherit the kingdom of God, for no unclean thing can dwell there, or dwell in his presence; for, in the language of Adam, Man of Holiness is his name, and the name of his Only Begotten is the Son of Man, even Jesus Christ, a righteous Judge, who shall come in the meridian of time.
verses.rendering: Wherefore teach it unto your children, that all men, everywhere, must repent, or they can in nowise inherit the kingdom of God, for no unclean thing can dwell there, or dwell in his presence; for, in the language of Adam, Man of Holiness is his name, and the name of his Only Begotten is the Son of Man, even Jesus Christ, a righteous Judge, who shall come in the meridian of time.
translator_notes: God's name in the Adamic language is 'Man of Holiness' — making 'Son of Man' literally 'Son of Man of Holiness,' i.e., Son of God. This is a remarkable etymological theology. Christ is named again as coming in the 'meridian of time.'

verses.text_source: Therefore I give unto you a commandment, to teach these things freely unto your children, saying:
verses.rendering: Therefore I give unto you a commandment, to teach these things freely unto your children, saying:
translator_notes: The command to teach is itself a commandment — parental instruction is obligatory.

verses.text_source: That by reason of transgression cometh the fall, which fall bringeth death, and inasmuch as ye were born into the world by water, and blood, and the spirit, which I have made, and so became of dust a living soul, even so ye must be born again into the kingdom of heaven, of water, and of the Spirit, and be cleansed by blood, even the blood of mine Only Begotten; that ye might be sanctified from all sin, and enjoy the words of eternal life in this world, and eternal life in the world to come, even immortal glory;
verses.rendering: That by reason of transgression cometh the fall, which fall bringeth death, and inasmuch as ye were born into the world by water, and blood, and the spirit, which I have made, and so became of dust a living soul, even so ye must be born again into the kingdom of heaven, of water, and of the Spirit, and be cleansed by blood, even the blood of mine Only Begotten; that ye might be sanctified from all sin, and enjoy the words of eternal life in this world, and eternal life in the world to come, even immortal glory;
translator_notes: The birth analogy: natural birth involves water, blood, and spirit; spiritual rebirth requires water (baptism), Spirit (Holy Ghost), and blood (Christ's Atonement). A trinitarian formula for salvation. Echoes John 3:5.

verses.text_source: For by the water ye keep the commandment; by the Spirit ye are justified, and by the blood ye are sanctified;
verses.rendering: For by the water ye keep the commandment; by the Spirit ye are justified, and by the blood ye are sanctified;
translator_notes: A concise soteriology: water = obedience (baptism), Spirit = justification, blood = sanctification.

verses.text_source: Therefore it is given to abide in you; the record of heaven; the Comforter; the peaceable things of immortal glory; the truth of all things; that which quickeneth all things, which maketh alive all things; that which knoweth all things, and hath all power according to wisdom, mercy, truth, justice, and judgment.
verses.rendering: Therefore it is given to abide in you; the record of heaven; the Comforter; the peaceable things of immortal glory; the truth of all things; that which quickeneth all things, which maketh alive all things; that which knoweth all things, and hath all power according to wisdom, mercy, truth, justice, and judgment.
translator_notes: A series of titles for the Holy Ghost: record of heaven, Comforter, truth of all things, quickener, omniscient, all-powerful. Echoes John 14:26 and Moroni 10:5.

verses.text_source: And now, behold, I say unto you: This is the plan of salvation unto all men, through the blood of mine Only Begotten, who shall come in the meridian of time.
verses.rendering: And now, behold, I say unto you: This is the plan of salvation unto all men, through the blood of mine Only Begotten, who shall come in the meridian of time.
translator_notes: The phrase 'plan of salvation' — a central LDS concept — is used explicitly. It is universal ('unto all men') and christocentric.

verses.text_source: And behold, all things have their likeness, and all things are created and made to bear record of me, both things which are temporal, and things which are spiritual; things which are in the heavens above, and things which are on the earth, and things which are in the earth, and things which are under the earth, both above and beneath: all things bear record of me.
verses.rendering: And behold, all things have their likeness, and all things are created and made to bear record of me, both things which are temporal, and things which are spiritual; things which are in the heavens above, and things which are on the earth, and things which are in the earth, and things which are under the earth, both above and beneath: all things bear record of me.
translator_notes: A cosmic testimony: all creation bears witness of God. The comprehensive list (heavens, earth, under the earth) echoes Philippians 2:10. Nature itself is revelatory.

verses.text_source: And it came to pass, when the Lord had spoken with Adam, our father, that Adam cried unto the Lord, and he was caught away by the Spirit of the Lord, and was carried down into the water, and was laid under the water, and was brought forth out of the water.
verses.rendering: And it came to pass, when the Lord had spoken with Adam, our father, that Adam cried unto the Lord, and he was caught away by the Spirit of the Lord, and was carried down into the water, and was laid under the water, and was brought forth out of the water.
translator_notes: Adam's baptism by immersion. The Spirit physically carries him into the water — a supernatural baptism. The immersion and emergence pattern mirrors Christian baptismal practice.

verses.text_source: And thus he was baptized, and the Spirit of God descended upon him, and thus he was born of the Spirit, and became quickened in the inner man.
verses.rendering: And thus he was baptized, and the Spirit of God descended upon him, and thus he was born of the Spirit, and became quickened in the inner man.
translator_notes: The Holy Ghost descends after baptism — the pattern of Christ's own baptism (Matt 3:16). 'Born of the Spirit' and 'quickened in the inner man' describe spiritual regeneration.

verses.text_source: And he heard a voice out of heaven, saying: Thou art baptized with fire, and with the Holy Ghost. This is the record of the Father, and the Son, from henceforth and forever;
verses.rendering: And he heard a voice out of heaven, saying: Thou art baptized with fire, and with the Holy Ghost. This is the record of the Father, and the Son, from henceforth and forever;
translator_notes: A heavenly voice confirms Adam's baptism of fire and the Holy Ghost — paralleling the voice at Jesus' baptism (Matt 3:17). 'The record of the Father, and the Son' — the gospel testimony.

verses.text_source: And thou art after the order of him who was without beginning of days or end of years, from all eternity to all eternity.
verses.rendering: And thou art after the order of him who was without beginning of days or end of years, from all eternity to all eternity.
translator_notes: Adam receives a priesthood order described in the same terms as Melchizedek's (Heb 7:3). This is the foundation of LDS Melchizedek Priesthood theology.

verses.text_source: Behold, thou art one in me, a son of God; and thus may all become my sons. Amen.
verses.rendering: Behold, thou art one in me, a son of God; and thus may all become my sons. Amen.
translator_notes: The climactic declaration: Adam is 'one in me, a son of God.' The invitation is universal: 'thus may all become my sons.' The chapter's doctrinal section closes with Amen.

verses.text_source: And Enoch lived sixty-five years, and begat Methuselah.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:21
verses.rendering: And Enoch lived sixty-five years, and begat Methuselah.
translator_notes: Returns to the genealogical framework of Gen 5:21, now after the extensive doctrinal interlude.

verses.text_source: And it came to pass that Enoch walked with God; and he was not, for God took him.
verses.text_reference: Genesis 5:24 — And Enoch walked with God: and he was not; for God took him.
verses.rendering: And it came to pass that Enoch walked with God; and he was not, for God took him.
translator_notes: Follows Gen 5:24 — one of the most mysterious verses in the Bible. Enoch's translation (being taken without dying) is stated simply. The full narrative is developed in Moses 7.
